arguments.task: Open Fanbasis official website and find the strongest official pages for API, developer, authentication, integrations, pricing, and terms/access. Prefer first-party documentation or official product pages. Read page content, not snippets. Return canonical URLs visited and concise extracted evidence for: product description, whether a public API exists, documented auth methods, pricing or access gating, and any rate limit references. If no official API/docs are found, explicitly say so and cite the official pages checked.

input.thought: Start a focused browser run on official Fanbasis sources to gather evidence from live first-party pages.

raw_result.description: Creator monetization platform for selling memberships, messages, digital products, and community access to fans.
raw_result.auth_notes: The official Terms mention Fanbasis and third-party integrations using application programming interfaces (APIs), but no first-party public developer/authentication documentation was found in the gathered evidence for a public API. No documented OAuth2, API key, Basic, or bearer/personal token scheme was found on the official pages reviewed.
raw_result.gating_reason: The gathered official pages did not expose a self-serve public developer API or developer signup flow. Evidence only shows the consumer/creator platform plus legal pages, so any API access appears non-self-serve or internal from the available evidence.

api_surface.breadth: No public first-party developer API documentation was found on the official Fanbasis pages reviewed; the Terms only reference APIs in the context of platform or third-party integrations, not a documented external API surface.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official MCP server or MCP documentation was found in the gathered first-party evidence.

raw_result.blocker: No usable public API documentation or documented public authentication method for external developers was found in the official evidence gathered.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: No public rate limit documentation was found on the official pages reviewed.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official Terms indicate the product supports third-party integrations and references APIs, but the evidence gathered does not show any public developer portal, API reference, auth guide, or pricing page for API access.
raw_result.human_review_reason: Structured fields are uncertain because the gathered evidence did not include a public developer/API page. In particular, auth_methods, access_tier, api_surface.type, and buildability_verdict are inferred from absence of public docs on the official pages reviewed rather than explicit developer documentation.
